notes: Interview questions for assembling an app spec when the user doesn't already have one. The skill procedure walks through these via AskUserQuestion. Each question maps to a section in spec-template.md.

questions.prompt: What's the name of this app, and what does it do? Give me one paragraph: who uses it, what's the core value, and what's the rough size (S/M/L)?
Do you have an empty app shell created in ODC Portal already? If yes, paste the asset key. If no, I'll walk you through creating one.
How should the app look? Pick the closest:
options: Apply OutSystems UI defaults (recommended for v1)
Reference an existing app's theme (provide app name)

questions.prompt: Who are the users? List each distinct role + a one-line description of what they can do. Examples: 'Customer (places orders, views their own history)', 'Manager (sees all orders, can refund)', 'Admin (manages users + system config)'. If anonymous access is intentional anywhere, mention that too.

What entities (data tables) does the app need? For each, give me: name, key attributes with types, and any foreign-key relationships. Example: 'Customer: Id (Long), Name (Text, 100), Email (Email, 256), CreatedAt (DateTime). Order: Id, CustomerId (FK to Customer), Total (Currency), Status (enum OrderStatus).' If you're not sure about types, use plain English — Mentor will translate.
What static enums / lookup values does the app use? List each enum + its values. Example: 'OrderStatus: Pending, Confirmed, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled'. If none, type 'None'.

What screens does the app need, AND which roles can access each one? This is the most important question — Mentor often defaults screens to public access if not told otherwise. Format as a list: 'Dashboard | landing + KPIs | All authenticated', 'OrderDetail | view/edit one order | Customer (own), Manager (all)'. Don't leave any screen without a role.

Any specific server actions you want? Format: 'GetOrdersForCustomer(CustomerId) → Order list. UpdateOrderStatus(OrderId, NewStatus) → success bool.' If you'd rather let Mentor derive them from the screens, just say 'derive from screens'.
Any external integrations? Examples: 'AI Model: outsystems_claude_4_6_sonnet (for ticket summary feature). REST: GET https://api.example.com/customers.' If none, type 'None' — don't leave blank.

What do you explicitly NOT want Mentor to build? This stops over-building. Examples: 'No public REST endpoints', 'No multi-tenant isolation', 'No admin UI for audit logs — server-side logging is fine'. If 'just build everything sensible', say so.

How will you know this build succeeded? Optional but useful. Examples: 'I can log in as Manager and see all orders', 'There are at least 5 example orders seeded'. Or 'skip — I'll just review the result manually'.
Anything else Mentor should know? Constraints, preferences, integration hints, customer color choices, etc. Optional.
